title: hypericum verrucosum
intro: Hypericum verrucosum, also known as rough St. John's wort, is a flowering plant species within the St. John's wort family native to Europe and parts of Asia. Its medicinal properties include the presence of hyperforin and hypericin, which have been traditionally used to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Hypericum verrucosum include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in soothing skin conditions, promoting wound healing, and reducing pain. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to exploit its therapeutic properties.
